name: Healthcare Organization Risk Fundamentals
:LABEL: Curriculum
description: Understand the fundamentals of healthcare organization risk management

name: Australian Healthcare Risk Management Regulatory Framework
:LABEL: Concept
understanding_criteria: Identify the Australian Commission on Safety and Quality in Health Care as the national body responsible for leading and coordinating improvements in safety and quality in health services;Describe how the National Safety and Quality Health Service Standards Second Edition provides the framework for healthcare organizations to assess and improve safety and quality;Explain the role of state and territory health departments in regulating healthcare services and enforcing compliance with incident reporting and investigation requirements;Map the eight NSQHS Standards to risk management activities including Clinical Governance, Partnering with Consumers, Preventing and Controlling Healthcare-Associated Infection, Medication Safety, Comprehensive Care, Communicating for Safety, Blood Management, and Recognising and Responding to Acute Deterioration;Describe how AS/NZS ISO 31000:2018 provides the principles, framework, and process for risk management in Australian healthcare organizations
description: Understanding australian healthcare risk management regulatory framework: Identify the Australian Commission on Safety and Quality in Health Care as the national body responsible for leading and coordinating improvements in safety and quality in health services

name: Clinical Governance and Risk Management Integration
:LABEL: Concept
understanding_criteria: Define clinical governance as the framework through which healthcare organizations are accountable for continuously improving quality and safeguarding standards of care;Explain how risk management functions as a key component within the broader clinical governance structure rather than as a separate activity;Describe the relationship between clinical governance committees and risk management activities including escalation pathways and reporting requirements;Identify the role of clinical governance advisors in coordinating risk management activities across clinical and administrative domains;Explain how the three lines model applies to healthcare governance including operational management, oversight functions, and independent assurance
description: Understanding clinical governance and risk management integration: Define clinical governance as the framework through which healthcare organizations are accountable for continuously improving quality and safeguarding standards of care

name: ISO 31000 Risk Management Process and Principles
:LABEL: Concept
understanding_criteria: Describe the ISO 31000 risk management process components including establishing context, risk identification, risk analysis, risk evaluation, risk treatment, and monitoring and review;Explain the principles of effective risk management including integration with organizational processes, structured and comprehensive approach, customization to context, inclusiveness, dynamic and responsive nature, and use of best available information;Apply the risk management framework components including leadership and commitment, integration into organizational processes, design, implementation, evaluation, and improvement;Distinguish between inherent risk and residual risk in the context of control effectiveness assessment;Create risk statements using the cause-event-consequence format to clearly articulate identified risks
description: Understanding iso 31000 risk management process and principles: Describe the ISO 31000 risk management process components including establishing context, risk identification, risk analysis, risk evaluation, risk treatment, and monitoring and review

name: Enterprise Risk Management Domains in Healthcare
:LABEL: Concept
understanding_criteria: List and describe the operational risk domain including workforce management, supply chain, access and patient flow, and service delivery processes;Explain clinical and patient safety risks including adverse events, clinical deterioration, medication errors, healthcare-associated infections, and blood management;Identify strategic risks including reputation, market position, accreditation status, and organizational sustainability;Describe financial risks including activity-based funding variations, private health insurer contracting, budget management, and insurance coverage;Explain human capital risks including workforce shortages, credentialing and scope of practice, mandatory reporting obligations, and workplace health and safety;Identify legal and regulatory risks including compliance with health services acts, privacy legislation, coronial reporting, and professional registration requirements;Describe technological risks including cybersecurity, information governance, electronic health record integrity, and digital health vendor management;Explain environmental and infrastructure risks including emergency preparedness, business continuity, and facility safety
description: Understanding enterprise risk management domains in healthcare: List and describe the operational risk domain including workforce management, supply chain, access and patient flow, and service delivery processes

name: Risk Identification and Assessment Techniques
:LABEL: Concept
understanding_criteria: Apply ISO 31010 risk assessment techniques appropriate to healthcare contexts including brainstorming, structured interviews, checklists, and scenario analysis;Engage diverse stakeholders in risk identification including patients, consumers, clinical staff, administrators, and external partners;Use organizational data sources to identify risks including incident reports, complaints, audit findings, clinical indicators, and workforce data;Identify emerging risks through environmental scanning of regulatory changes, technology developments, and healthcare system trends;Describe how consumer partnership in risk identification aligns with NSQHS Standard 2 Partnering with Consumers
description: Understanding risk identification and assessment techniques: Apply ISO 31010 risk assessment techniques appropriate to healthcare contexts including brainstorming, structured interviews, checklists, and scenario analysis

name: Risk Analysis and Prioritization Methods
:LABEL: Concept
understanding_criteria: Apply the organizational risk matrix to rate risks based on consequence and likelihood using defined criteria;Distinguish between inherent risk rating before controls and residual risk rating after considering existing controls;Assess control effectiveness by evaluating design adequacy and operating effectiveness of existing risk treatments;Create risk heat maps to visualize risk profiles and communicate priorities to governance committees and executive leadership;Determine risk acceptance, treatment, or escalation decisions based on organizational risk appetite and tolerance levels;Populate risk register entries including risk description, risk owner, inherent rating, controls, residual rating, and treatment plan
description: Understanding risk analysis and prioritization methods: Apply the organizational risk matrix to rate risks based on consequence and likelihood using defined criteria

name: Australian Incident Management and Severity Assessment
:LABEL: Concept
understanding_criteria: Apply the Severity Assessment Code framework to classify incidents from SAC1 extreme risk to SAC4 low risk based on actual and potential consequences;Identify state and territory incident management systems including NSW Health Incident Information Management System, Victorian Health Incident Management System, and equivalent jurisdictional frameworks;Describe mandatory reporting requirements to state health departments for serious adverse events and SAC1 incidents;Explain reportable incidents requirements specific to aged care services under the Serious Incident Response Scheme;Identify NDIS incident reporting obligations for healthcare organizations providing disability services;Describe triggers for coronial reporting including deaths in healthcare settings, deaths during or following procedures, and unexpected deaths
description: Understanding australian incident management and severity assessment: Apply the Severity Assessment Code framework to classify incidents from SAC1 extreme risk to SAC4 low risk based on actual and potential consequences

name: Root Cause Analysis and Systems-Based Investigation
:LABEL: Concept
understanding_criteria: Describe the RCA2 methodology as a contemporary systems-based approach to investigating serious incidents in Australian healthcare;Apply Human Factors and Systems Engineering principles to understand how system design contributes to incidents;Distinguish between active failures as immediate errors and latent conditions as underlying system weaknesses;Use investigation frameworks to identify contributing factors across domains including organizational factors, work environment, team factors, individual staff factors, task factors, and patient characteristics;Develop actionable recommendations that address system-level causes rather than focusing solely on individual performance;Explain when to use Root Cause Analysis for serious incidents versus After Action Review for rapid learning or clinical review for clinical care concerns
description: Understanding root cause analysis and systems-based investigation: Describe the RCA2 methodology as a contemporary systems-based approach to investigating serious incidents in Australian healthcare

name: Prospective Risk Assessment Using FMEA
:LABEL: Concept
understanding_criteria: Describe Failure Mode and Effects Analysis as a prospective tool to identify potential failures before they occur;Apply FMEA methodology to new processes, technologies, or service changes by identifying failure modes, causes, and effects;Calculate risk priority numbers based on severity, occurrence, and detection ratings;Develop preventive actions and process improvements based on FMEA findings;Distinguish between prospective risk assessment using FMEA and retrospective investigation using RCA
description: Understanding prospective risk assessment using fmea: Describe Failure Mode and Effects Analysis as a prospective tool to identify potential failures before they occur

name: Open Disclosure and Duty of Candour
:LABEL: Concept
understanding_criteria: Define open disclosure as the process of open and honest communication with patients and families when things go wrong in healthcare;Apply the Australian Open Disclosure Framework principles including acknowledgement, truthfulness, timeliness, apology or expression of regret, support, and integrated risk management and systems improvement;Describe the legal and ethical obligations for healthcare providers to disclose adverse events to patients and families in a timely manner;Develop an open disclosure plan for a serious adverse event including initial response, investigation updates, and final discussion of findings and improvements;Explain how effective open disclosure reduces litigation risk, maintains patient trust, and supports organizational learning;Document open disclosure processes in accordance with organizational policy and NSQHS Standard 1 Clinical Governance requirements
description: Understanding open disclosure and duty of candour: Define open disclosure as the process of open and honest communication with patients and families when things go wrong in healthcare

name: Just Culture and Safety Culture Principles
:LABEL: Concept
understanding_criteria: Define just culture as an organizational culture that balances accountability with learning from errors and system improvement;Distinguish between human error requiring consolation and system review, at-risk behavior requiring coaching and removal of incentives for risk, and reckless behavior requiring remedial or disciplinary action;Describe how safety culture promotes reporting and learning rather than blame and punishment;Identify strategies for building just culture including leadership commitment, transparent communication, fair and consistent responses to incidents, and protection for staff who report safety concerns;Explain the relationship between just culture and incident reporting rates as a measure of organizational safety maturity
description: Understanding just culture and safety culture principles: Define just culture as an organizational culture that balances accountability with learning from errors and system improvement

name: Near Miss Reporting and Learning Systems
:LABEL: Concept
understanding_criteria: Define near misses and good catches as situations where harm was prevented through early detection or intervention;Explain why near miss reporting provides valuable opportunities to identify and address system vulnerabilities before serious harm occurs;Develop organizational processes that encourage reporting of near misses through accessible reporting mechanisms and non-punitive responses;Analyze near miss data to identify patterns, trends, and opportunities for preventive actions;Implement feedback mechanisms to close the loop with staff who report near misses and demonstrate how their reports led to improvements
description: Understanding near miss reporting and learning systems: Define near misses and good catches as situations where harm was prevented through early detection or intervention

name: Risk Registers and Treatment Planning
:LABEL: Concept
understanding_criteria: Maintain organizational risk registers at enterprise level and clinical service line level with consistent taxonomy and structure;Assign risk owners with accountability for monitoring and treating specific risks;Develop risk treatment plans specifying actions, responsibilities, timelines, and resources required to reduce risk to acceptable levels;Define key risk indicators and key performance indicators to monitor risk levels and treatment effectiveness over time;Establish review cycles for risk registers to ensure currency and relevance of risk information;Link risk register entries to assurance evidence including audit results, incident data, and compliance monitoring
description: Understanding risk registers and treatment planning: Maintain organizational risk registers at enterprise level and clinical service line level with consistent taxonomy and structure

name: Risk Appetite and Tolerance Framework
:LABEL: Concept
understanding_criteria: Define risk appetite as the amount and type of risk an organization is willing to accept in pursuit of its strategic objectives;Explain risk tolerance as the acceptable variation in performance relative to achieving specific objectives;Develop organizational risk appetite statements aligned with strategic goals and values for different risk categories;Translate risk appetite into delegations of authority and escalation thresholds for operational decision-making;Apply risk appetite criteria to determine when risks require acceptance by executive leadership or board versus management-level treatment;Link risk appetite to resource allocation decisions and strategic planning processes
description: Understanding risk appetite and tolerance framework: Define risk appetite as the amount and type of risk an organization is willing to accept in pursuit of its strategic objectives

name: Governance Reporting and Board Assurance
:LABEL: Concept
understanding_criteria: Prepare risk reports for clinical governance committees including risk profile summaries, emerging risks, and treatment progress;Develop board-level risk reporting that highlights strategic risks, risk appetite alignment, and assurance gaps;Create assurance maps that identify sources of assurance for key risks across three lines of defense;Present risk information using heat maps, trend analysis, and key risk indicators to support governance decision-making;Explain the interface between risk management and internal audit functions in providing independent assurance;Document risk governance structures including committee terms of reference, reporting lines, and decision-making authorities
description: Understanding governance reporting and board assurance: Prepare risk reports for clinical governance committees including risk profile summaries, emerging risks, and treatment progress

name: Complaints and Consumer Feedback as Risk Signals
:LABEL: Concept
understanding_criteria: Analyze patient and family complaints to identify patterns indicating systemic risks or service quality issues;Develop procedures for documenting and responding to complaints that promote patient satisfaction and reduce litigation risk;Establish response timeframes, staff responsibilities, and prescribed actions for different complaint severity levels;Integrate complaint data with incident data and other risk indicators to provide comprehensive risk intelligence;Apply NSQHS Standard 2 Partnering with Consumers requirements to complaint management and feedback processes;Use consumer feedback to co-design risk mitigation strategies and service improvements
description: Understanding complaints and consumer feedback as risk signals: Analyze patient and family complaints to identify patterns indicating systemic risks or service quality issues

name: Mandatory Reporting and Legal Obligations
:LABEL: Concept
understanding_criteria: Identify AHPRA mandatory reporting obligations for notifiable conduct and impairment of registered health practitioners;Describe Therapeutic Goods Administration reporting requirements for medical device incidents and adverse events following immunization;Explain Privacy Act 1988 requirements and Notifiable Data Breaches scheme obligations for health information security incidents;Identify state and territory mandatory reporting requirements for child protection and family violence;Describe workplace health and safety notification requirements for serious injuries, illnesses, and dangerous incidents;Explain credentialing and scope of practice processes as risk controls for clinical practice
description: Understanding mandatory reporting and legal obligations: Identify AHPRA mandatory reporting obligations for notifiable conduct and impairment of registered health practitioners

name: Risk Management Information Systems and Data Quality
:LABEL: Concept
understanding_criteria: Describe how Risk Management Information Systems provide platforms for documenting incidents, tracking risks, and reporting trends;Generate reports from RMIS including incident summaries, open claims, risk register status, and benchmarking data;Ensure data quality in risk and incident reporting systems through standardized taxonomies, validation rules, and governance processes;Develop dashboards for real-time monitoring of key risk indicators and incident trends;Apply privacy and security requirements to risk management data including access controls and de-identification where appropriate;Integrate RMIS data with other organizational systems including quality improvement databases and clinical information systems
description: Understanding risk management information systems and data quality: Describe how Risk Management Information Systems provide platforms for documenting incidents, tracking risks, and reporting trends

name: Business Continuity and Emergency Management
:LABEL: Concept
understanding_criteria: Develop contingency plans for system-wide failures including electronic health record outages, cybersecurity incidents, and data breaches;Align emergency preparedness planning with Australasian Inter-service Incident Management System principles and state emergency management arrangements;Prepare business continuity plans for pandemic events, natural disasters, prolonged power loss, and mass casualty incidents;Establish incident management structures and communication protocols for activating emergency response;Test business continuity and emergency plans through exercises and incorporate lessons learned into plan updates;Coordinate emergency planning with external agencies including health departments, emergency services, and neighboring healthcare facilities
description: Understanding business continuity and emergency management: Develop contingency plans for system-wide failures including electronic health record outages, cybersecurity incidents, and data breaches

name: Risk Management Plan as Living Document
:LABEL: Concept
understanding_criteria: Develop a comprehensive Risk Management Plan that defines purpose, scope, objectives, governance structure, and processes;Define roles and responsibilities for risk management including risk owners, risk coordinators, committee members, and executive accountability;Establish education and training requirements including new employee orientation, ongoing competency development, annual review, and event-specific training;Create communication protocols for risk information sharing including reporting channels, escalation pathways, and feedback mechanisms;Build in provisions for regular review and update of the Risk Management Plan based on emerging risks, lessons learned, regulatory changes, and organizational changes;Link the Risk Management Plan to NSQHS accreditation evidence requirements and quality improvement activities;Ensure the plan promotes a safe, no-blame culture through anonymous reporting capabilities and just culture principles
description: Understanding risk management plan as living document: Develop a comprehensive Risk Management Plan that defines purpose, scope, objectives, governance structure, and processes
